content.text:
Memory stage: NoteConstructor v1.
        Current time: <instant>
        Timezone: Asia/Jerusalem
        Namespace: project:<0>

        Router decision: MIXED
        Router memory types: CLAIM, NOTE, SOURCE, ENTITY
        Router reason: Document ingest uses deterministic document truth route; stable document facts are extracted as claims, notes/sources keep richer context, and action items stay explicit-only.

        Stage instructions:
        You are NoteConstructor v1 for a long-term AI agent.

Goal:
Construct reusable semantic notes from TARGET_MESSAGE when the material is contextual rather than a clean atomic fact.

A note is for decisions, rationale, design direction, plans, hypotheses, lessons, trade-offs, troubleshooting procedures, and compact document/context digests.
A note must add reusable meaning. It must not be a generic summary of the target turn.

Return JSON:
{
  "notes": [
    {
      "title": "short English title",
      "summary": "self-contained English semantic fragment",
      "note_type": "decision | direction | hypothesis | plan | lesson | doc_digest | context",
      "scope_json": {
        "kind": "global | conversation | entity | environment | document | project",
        "text": "human-readable applicability boundary",
        "basis": "explicit | inferred | summarized | imported"
      },
      "entity_refs": [
        {"entity_id": "resolved-entity-id", "role": "primary | secondary | mentioned | owner | subject"}
      ],
      "keywords": ["short search keyword"],
      "tags": ["short_tag"],
      "candidate_claim_hints": [],
      "confidence": 0.0,
      "importance": 1,
      "valid_from": "ISO-8601 or null",
      "valid_to": "ISO-8601 or null",
      "evidence_quote": "exact substring from TARGET_MESSAGE",
      "evidence_kind": "direct | summarized | imported | inferred | derived_from_note",
      "evidence_reason": "why this source supports the note",
      "rationale": "why this should be a note, not a claim"
    }
  ]
}

Rules:
- Write title, summary, keywords, tags, evidence_reason, and rationale in English.
- Preserve proper names, product names, repo names, file names, exact commands, exact identifiers, dates, and quoted terms.
- Use only entity IDs listed in Resolved entities.
- Never write raw labels such as "user", "assistant", "project", or "document" into entity_refs. If a note is about the user, use the resolved USER entity id from Resolved entities.
- For project-level notes without a more specific subject, use the resolved namespace/project subject entity.
- Use TARGET_MESSAGE as the only evidence. Earlier messages are context only.
- evidence_quote must be an exact short substring copied from TARGET_MESSAGE source data.
- Do not construct notes from greetings, phatic chatter, pure questions, one-off commands with no durable rationale, or memory mechanics discussion that contains no reusable project/user decision.
- Do not construct notes from a single weak uncertain factual observation when source recall is enough and there is no rationale, decision, plan, lesson, or reusable analysis.
- Do not duplicate a claim candidate as a note when a precise claim is enough.
- Do use a note when the target captures "why", "how", "we decided", "current approach", trade-offs, working agreements, troubleshooting order, or a design direction.
- If TARGET_MESSAGE is a document or document section, preserve document title/source/section names and create section-scoped notes for important definitions, component roles, workflows, policies, and lists.
- For dense technical documents and prompt packs, prefer several strong section-scoped notes over one broad document summary. Keep only reusable notes; do not summarize every paragraph.
- For one document section, return at most two notes. If a section defines one compact component or policy, return one note.
- If source metadata force_memory_write=true, create a note when the content is reusable context but not a clean claim/action item/profile update.
- For named components, include the exact component names in title, summary, keywords, or tags so later retrieval can find the component role.
- A troubleshooting/procedure note is allowed, but do not create an action item unless the target creates a commitment.
- Keep each note self-contained: future recall should understand it without the original conversation.
- Keep summary concise: usually one or two sentences.
- Prefer zero notes over broad summary spam.
- Return valid JSON only.

        Relevant persisted memory for dedup/context only:
        - claim hot-path:claim:<0> has_constraint; Repair addresses memory degradation including duplicate clusters, stale items, contradictory active items, note spam, profile drift, and bad merges.; scope=agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md section 3 maintenance paths
- note hot-path:note:<0> DOC_DIGEST; `v3` memory architecture uses four axes to avoid brittle or dump-like memory; In `v3`, memory is organized along four axes—Representation (Source/Claim/Note/Profile/Task/Episode), Lifecycle (write→reconcile→consolidate→repair→retain/archive/delete), Retrieval policy (claim-first/note-first/mixed/evidence-first with bounded budgets), and Scope & lineage (scope_json/scope_text/source refs/origin note/memory runs). The document states all four are necessary to prevent transcript-dump or brittle fact-store failure modes.; scope=Applies to section '01. Architecture / 2. Центральная архитектурная идея' in agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md
- claim hot-path:claim:<1> responsible_for; Memory worker or manager is responsible for routing, retrieval-before-update, canonicalization, note/claim extraction, reconciliation, consolidation, repair, and retention in the current project architecture.; scope=agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md section 10.2
- claim hot-path:claim:<2> has_constraint; Consolidation promotes mature notes into claims, tasks, profile updates, or episodes when the signal becomes strong enough.; scope=agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md section 3 maintenance paths
- note hot-path:note:<1> DOC_DIGEST; Update path is not append-only and requires retrieval/reconciliation; The architecture defines memory writes as an update workflow, not blind append: decide whether to write, retrieve similar/conflicting items, produce typed candidates, and apply policies (`insert`, `update`, `supersede`, `retract`, `noop`). Skipping retrieval/reconciliation causes quality degradation such as duplicates, stale preferences, conflicts, task explosion, and summary spam.; scope=Applies to section '01. Architecture / 9. Главная мысль: update не должен быть append-only' in agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md
- claim hot-path:claim:<3> has_constraint; Both claim and note should be written when material contains explicit facts or tasks plus rationale or direction.; scope=Applies to agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md section 7 guidance
- claim hot-path:claim:<4> has_goal; The system goal is for the memory layer to store facts, preferences, tasks, rationale, and evolving plans.; scope=Applies to the architecture described in agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md (section 1).
- claim hot-path:claim:<5> has_constraint; Tier A core in-context memory should include a short user profile, active tasks, a very short session summary, and system-level behavior preferences.; scope=Applies to the architecture described in agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md, section 5.
- note hot-path:note:<2> DOC_DIGEST; Recommended pipeline routes routed writes through canonicalization, specialized updaters, reconciliation, and storage; In the recommended architecture, Memory Router outcomes (noop/direct_structured_write/note_write/mixed) feed Write-Time Retrieval and Entity Canonicalizer, then specialized components (Claim Extractor, Task Updater, Profile Updater, Note Constructor) and reconcilers write to Postgres + pgvector, with background consolidation/repair/retention workers and runtime retrieval-answer loop.; scope=Applies to section '01. Architecture / 4. Рекомендуемая архитектура' in agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md
- claim hot-path:claim:<6> has_constraint; Truth state and retrieval state are separate concepts and must not be mixed.; scope=Applies to agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md section 8 policy
- claim hot-path:claim:<7> has_constraint; Tier A core in-context memory should not include all claims, all notes, raw transcript, or long lists of facts.; scope=Applies to the architecture described in agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md, section 5.
- claim hot-path:claim:<8> has_constraint; A note should be written when information is useful for future retrieval.; scope=Applies to agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md section 7 guidance
- claim hot-path:claim:<9> has_constraint; Tier C rich note memory serves as an intermediate semantic layer for design direction, rationale, local conclusions, plan fragments, document digests, and lessons.; scope=Applies to the architecture described in agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md, section 5.
- note hot-path:note:<3> DOC_DIGEST; Truth state and retrieval state are separate axes; The document requires keeping truth status separate from retrieval temperature: truth state tracks epistemic validity (with different allowed values for claims vs notes), while retrieval state tracks hot-path/search handling; items can be resolved or superseded yet remain warm for rationale or historical evidence.; scope=Applies to section '01. Architecture / 8. Truth state и retrieval state' in agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md
- claim hot-path:claim:<10> has_goal; The system goal is for the memory layer to support updating knowledge rather than only accumulating it.; scope=Applies to the architecture described in agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md (section 1).
- claim hot-path:claim:<11> has_constraint; The memory system must not convert every turn into permanent knowledge.; scope=Applies to the architecture described in agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md (section 1).
- claim hot-path:claim:<12> has_constraint; Memory updates in the current project must not be append-only.; scope=agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md section 9

        Resolved entities:
        - mention=Memory repair; action=LINK_EXISTING; id=entity:<0>; type=existing; name=Memory repair; reason=The section describes repair as a distinct workflow, matching existing service 'Memory Repair Worker'.
- mention=Note path; action=LINK_EXISTING; id=entity:<1>; type=existing; name=Note path; reason=Referenced in retrieved architecture context as a named concept; safe match to existing 'Note path' concept.
- mention=current project; action=CREATE_NEW; id=entity:<2>; type=PROJECT; name=Current project; reason=Default current-project subject for claims without a more specific resolved subject

Document title: 01. Architecture
Document source: agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md
Document section: 01. Architecture / 13. Note maturity .. 14. Memory repair
Document lines: 467-508

## 13. Note maturity

Чтобы `Note` не превратился в summary-store, нужен lifecycle зрелости.

Полезный паттерн:
- `fresh`
- `stabilizing`
- `mature`
- `consolidated`

Что может повышать maturity:
- note использовался в retrieval
- note подтвержден несколькими sources
- note не конфликтует с новыми данными
- note пережил какое-то время без supersede
- note важен для recurring rationale questions

Что может понижать или закрывать note:
- явный supersede
- устаревший scope
- отсутствие использования
- слабая поддержка evidence

## 14. Memory repair

Repair — это отдельный background workflow.

Что он чинит:
- duplicate notes / claims
- contradictory active items
- profile drift
- stale task states
- bad entity merges
- note spam
- budget leakage в retrieval

Почему это надо выделять отдельно:
- consolidation делает память богаче
- repair делает память здоровее

Это разные работы.

responseFormat.description: Construct contextual memory note candidates from one target message.
responseFormat.name: memory_note_constructor

systemPrompts:
You are a concise assistant inside a Gromozeka memory e2e test.
Answer user questions directly.
Do not use tools.
If the question asks for only a short value, return only that value.

## 13. Note maturity

Чтобы `Note` не превратился в summary-store, нужен lifecycle зрелости.

Полезный паттерн:
- `fresh`
- `stabilizing`
- `mature`

Что может повышать maturity:
- note использовался в retrieval
- note подтвержден несколькими sources
- note не конфликтует с новыми данными
- note пережил какое-то время без supersede

Что может понижать или закрывать note:
- явный supersede
- устаревший scope
- отсутствие использования
- слабая поддержка evidence

## 14. Memory repair

Repair — это отдельный background workflow.

Что он чинит:
- duplicate notes / claims
- contradictory active items
- profile drift
- stale task states
- bad entity merges
- note spam
- budget leakage в retrieval

Почему это надо выделять отдельно:
- consolidation делает память богаче
- repair делает память здоровее

Это разные работы.

Note maturity' in agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md"},"summary":"The architecture defines a note maturity lifecycle (`fresh` → `stabilizing` → `mature` → `consolidated`) so `Note` memory remains curated instead of degrading into a summary-store; maturity is raised by reuse, multi-source support, non-conflict with new data, time stability without supersede, and recurring rationale relevance.","tags":["memory_lifecycle","note_quality","doc_section_digest"],"title":"Note maturity lifecycle prevents summary-store drift","valid_from":"1970-01-01T00:00:00Z","valid_to":null},{"candidate_claim_hints":[],"confidence":0.97,"entity_refs":[{"entity_id":"entity:<0>","role":"primary"}],"evidence_kind":"direct","evidence_quote":"Repair — это отдельный background workflow.","evidence_reason":"The section directly defines Repair as a separate workflow and lists the specific failure modes it addresses, then contrasts it with consolidation.","importance":5,"keywords":["Memory repair","consolidation","background workflow","duplicate notes","contradictory active items"],"note_type":"doc_digest","rationale":"This captures durable architectural separation and intent (why repair exists separately), which is better stored as contextual note than a narrow claim.","scope_json":{"basis":"explicit","kind":"document","text":"Applies to section '01. Architecture / 14. Memory repair' in agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md"},"summary":"`Memory repair` is defined as a separate background workflow focused on memory health issues (duplicates, contradictions, profile drift, stale tasks, bad merges, note spam, retrieval budget leakage), while consolidation has a different role of enriching memory; they are explicitly different jobs.","tags":["repair_workflow","memory_health","doc_section_digest"],"title":"Memory repair is a distinct workflow from consolidation","valid_from":"1970-01-01T00:00:00Z","valid_to":null}]}
